bs: Two reputable sources disagree on big-three combined cloud share (Synergy Q3 2025 = 63%; CRN-reported Q4 2025 = 68%) and on the per-vendor split (29/20/13 vs 28/21/15). Likely different metrics (CIS vs total enterprise cloud spend) and quarters, but unreconciled.
qr: cloud infrastructure market share Q4 2025 AWS Azure Google Synergy Research percent
fnd: Anchored on Synergy Q3 (scraped primary-ish) for the map; CRN Q4 noted as a possibly-newer, possibly-different-metric figure.
cav: Single quarter from one analyst firm; did not reconcile the metric definitions or pull Canalys/IDC as a third triangulation point.

bs: Egress and hot-storage per-GB figures ($0.09/GB AWS, $0.0085/GB OCI, hot $0.018-0.023) come from one FinOps-vendor blog, not the AWS/OCI/Azure/GCP official pricing pages.
qr: AWS Azure Google Cloud data egress pricing per GB 2025 comparison USD
fnd: Figures are consistent with widely-cited standard rates and corroborated across search snippets (CloudBolt, emma.ms), but not primary-verified.
cav: Vendor blog = tertiary; published cloud rates change and have regional/tiered exceptions not captured. No primary pricing-page scrape.

bs: Cloud LLM per-token rates are from a third-party aggregator's April 2026 snapshot, not the providers' own pricing pages, and LLM rates drift monthly.
qr: LLM API pricing per million tokens 2026 GPT Claude Gemini input output cost comparison
fnd: Coherent rate card (GPT-5 $1.25/$10, Opus 4.6 $15/$75, cheap tier $0.10/1M); flagged hr:m for drift.
cav: Aggregator = secondary; a fact-check against OpenAI/Anthropic/Google pricing pages was not done this pass. Prices likely already stale within weeks.

bs: The headline inference crossover ($7.15/1M local vs $6.90/1M OpenAI) is a TCO MODEL on 50M-tok/day datacenter hardware (mid-2025 prices), not a measured deployment, and not the consumer-device curve our OS actually runs on.
qr: on-device LLM inference cost vs cloud API price per million tokens 2025 Apple Neural Engine local
fnd: Useful directional crossover + sensitivity (EU power doubles break-even; refresh at 24-30mo adds $130-200k); flagged op:1 + hr:m.
cav: Modeled, single source, datacenter-scale. The consumer-device (Apple Neural Engine / M-series unified memory) $/token curve - the one that matters for our router - is ungrounded.

bs: No grounded data on real on-device inference throughput/cost for a baseline consumer device running our token mix and latency target (the input the hybrid-router cost model depends on).
fnd: Not searched this pass - deferred; sitepoint covers datacenter GPUs not Apple Neural Engine / Snapdragon NPU consumer inference.
cav: This is the single highest-leverage unknown for the wm-ad-hybrid-router directive; until grounded, the router cost model is theoretical.

bs: Apple Private Cloud Compute and Apple/Google on-device foundation-model third-party access terms (rate limits, attestation, first-party carve-outs) are ungrounded - the basis for the dc-cloud-3 'inference-layer gate' claim.
fnd: Not searched this pass; flagged as gap wm-cl-g-apple-pcc and as distinctive claim dc-cloud-3 (low confidence 0.48).
cav: Distinctive claim rests on an unverified premise; next wave should scrape Apple Foundation Models framework + Android AICore terms.

bs: The '10x/yr (~1000x/3yr) inference cost decline' trend is community/Reddit-sourced (tertiary), used only as directional support.
fnd: Capped at c:0.50, st:T; not load-bearing for any directive.
cav: Tertiary; the original a16z/blog primary was not pulled. Treat as ordinal hint only.

bs: Across the world-map, most facts rest on ONE source per claim; few carry 2+ independent primary sources. The map is wide but thinly triangulated, so correlated source error would not be caught.
fnd: Observed while reconciling notes/* this pass; distribution and regulation are best-grounded (primary law/dev docs), cloud and the SmartScreen tertiary cluster are weakest.
cav: PROTOCOL principle 7 (base rates / triangulation) is under-applied at the factual-sweep tier; the dialectic (T9) is meant to catch this but has only run on a subset.

s: claimed 'real research NOT STARTED' while notes/ and claims/ were populated
bs: HANDOFF.md drifted out of sync with reality - it asserted zero waves had run while T1-T7 + partial T8-T13 were already in notes/claims. Process blindspot: docs and data have no freshness/owner link.
fnd: Reconciled state manually from git + file inspection; refreshing HANDOFF this pass.
cav: Risk that a future instance trusts a stale cold-start doc over the actual artifacts. Needs a 'last-reconciled' stamp or a generated status.

s: AI Act Art.6 / Annex III high-risk boundary
bs: Whether mainstream OS AI-assistant features count as Annex III high-risk under AI Act Art.6 is genuinely unsettled in law (pending delegated acts/guidance) - the dc-reg-2 prediction rests on an open legal question, not a resolved one.
fnd: Primary law text grounded, but classification outcome is forward-looking; flagged as gap wm-reg-gap-euai-highrisk-boundary-for-os-ai.
cav: Honest unresolved: this is a real-world unknown, not a research shortfall - cannot be closed by more scraping, only by Commission action over time.

bs: The proposed language-kernel ABI (signed manifests, lexicon grid, candidate sets, brand/tenant overlays) is architecture-elicited from local framework docs and repo context; it is not yet grounded by live implementation evidence or cross-surface interoperability tests.
qr: read local framework docs Untitled document-9.md and Translation.xml
inspect logicalworks vision artifacts and sales-landing-page locale seam
fnd: Strong conceptual fit with the OS-layer framing, but no measured proof yet that the ABI is the minimal right cut or that enterprise white-label constraints map cleanly onto it.
cav: Architecture recommendation is ahead of evidence; risk is world-building outrunning measured substrate needs.

bs: The language's core security claim — 'state-caging' (formal-verification-in-bytecode enforcing immutable execution boundaries) + 'stack-shadowing' — rests on tertiary citations (a malware-tools blog, the WASM-security wiki). HVM/interaction-combinators are real but young; security-as-bytecode-invariant is aspirational, not demonstrated at scale.
fnd: Strong conceptual fit (no-global-state removes whole bug classes) but the specific 'state-caging' mechanism has no primary/peer-reviewed grounding.
cav: Do not assert security-by-bytecode as proven. Falsifier: no production interaction-net runtime ships formal-verification at the bytecode layer with acceptable latency.

s: WebGPU cross-platform, Safari lacks compute shaders
bs: The compute-fabric research assumes a WebGPU/WASM (web) substrate, but Canvas is native-macOS-primary (Swift/Metal). WebGPU is cross-platform but Safari lacks compute shaders; Metal is Apple-native and non-portable. The language's execution-substrate choice (web vs native; WebGPU vs Metal vs wgpu) is unresolved and consequential.
fnd: wgpu (Rust; runs on Vulkan/Metal/DX12/WASM; <5% overhead) is a candidate bridge that fits Canvas's Rust backend and could reconcile native+web.
cav: Choosing web-first contradicts the native-primary architecture (canvas-architecture.md); choosing Metal-only loses the web tier. Decide before committing the substrate.

bs: Potential drift between technical documentation and actual implementation of security controls in the 11 engines.
qr: security control documentation drift AI agents
fnd: AI-generated documentation often masks absence of actionable content with verbosity.

bs: Unknown adoption rate of A2A protocol by external agent vendors, threatening cross-platform interoperability.
fnd: Early standard; vendor buy-in is uncertain.
